name: class11_flutter
description: "Kinetic Insight Flutter frontend prototype."
# The following line prevents the package from being accidentally published to
# pub.dev using `flutter pub publish`. This is preferred for private packages.
publish_to: 'none' # Remove this line if you wish to publish to pub.dev

# The following defines the version and build number for your application.
# A version number is three numbers separated by dots, like 1.2.43
# followed by an optional build number separated by a +.
# Both the version and the builder number may be overridden in flutter
# build by specifying --build-name and --build-number, respectively.
# In Android, build-name is used as versionName while build-number used as versionCode.
# Read more about Android versioning at https://developer.android.com/studio/publish/versioning
# In iOS, build-name is used as CFBundleShortVersionString while build-number is used as CFBundleVersion.
# Read more about iOS versioning at
# https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/documentation/General/Reference/InfoPlistKeyReference/Articles/CoreFoundationKeys.html
# In Windows, build-name is used as the major, minor, and patch parts
# of the product and file versions while build-number is used as the build suffix.
version: 1.0.0+1

environment:
  sdk: ^3.9.2

# Dependencies specify other packages that your package needs in order to work.
# To automatically upgrade your package dependencies to the latest versions
# consider running `flutter pub upgrade --major-versions`. Alternatively,
# dependencies can be manually updated by changing the version numbers below to
# the latest version available on pub.dev. To see which dependencies have newer
# versions available, run `flutter pub outdated`.
dependencies:
  flutter:
    sdk: flutter

  # The following adds the Cupertino Icons font to your application.
  # Use with the CupertinoIcons class for iOS style icons.
  cupertino_icons: ^1.0.8

dev_dependencies:
  flutter_test:
    sdk: flutter

  # The "flutter_lints" package below contains a set of recommended lints to
  # encourage good coding practices. The lint set provided by the package is
  # activated in the `analysis_options.yaml` file located at the root of your

  flutter_lints: ^5.0.0

# For information on the generic Dart part of this file, see the

# The following section is specific to Flutter packages.
flutter:

  # included with your application, so that you can use the icons in
  # the material Icons class.
  uses-material-design: true

  # To add assets to your application, add an assets section, like this:
  # assets:
  #   - images/a_dot_burr.jpeg

  # An image asset can refer to one or more resolution-specific "variants", see
  # https://flutter.dev/to/resolution-aware-images

  # For details regarding adding assets from package dependencies, see
  # https://flutter.dev/to/asset-from-package

  # To add custom fonts to your application, add a fonts section here,
  # in this "flutter" section. Each entry in this list should have a
  # "family" key with the font family name, and a "fonts" key with a
  # list giving the asset and other descriptors for the font. For
  # example:
  # fonts:
  #   - family: Schyler
  #     fonts:
  #       - asset: fonts/Schyler-Regular.ttf
  #       - asset: fonts/Schyler-Italic.ttf
  #         style: italic
  #   - family: Trajan Pro
  #     fonts:
  #       - asset: fonts/TrajanPro.ttf
  #       - asset: fonts/TrajanPro_Bold.ttf
  #         weight: 700

  # For details regarding fonts from package dependencies,
  # see https://flutter.dev/to/font-from-package

535: class _MirrorPainter extends CustomPainter {
536:   _MirrorPainter(this.result);
537: 
538:   final SphericalMirrorResult result;
539: 
540:   @override
541:   void paint(Canvas canvas, Size size) {
542:     final cy = size.height * 0.62;
543:     final mirrorX = size.width * 0.72;
544:     canvas.drawLine(
545:       Offset(0, cy),
546:       Offset(size.width, cy),
547:       Paint()
548:         ..color = const Color(0xFF94A3B8)
549:         ..strokeWidth = 2,
550:     );
551: 
552:     final mirrorPath = Path();
553:     final mirrorTop = Offset(mirrorX, cy - size.height * 0.33);
554:     final mirrorBottom = Offset(mirrorX, cy + size.height * 0.22);
555:     if (result.type == MirrorType.concave) {
556:       mirrorPath
557:         ..moveTo(mirrorTop.dx, mirrorTop.dy)
558:         ..quadraticBezierTo(
559:           mirrorX + 42,
560:           cy - 12,
561:           mirrorBottom.dx,
562:           mirrorBottom.dy,
563:         );
564:     } else {
565:       mirrorPath
566:         ..moveTo(mirrorTop.dx, mirrorTop.dy)
567:         ..quadraticBezierTo(
568:           mirrorX - 42,
569:           cy - 12,
570:           mirrorBottom.dx,
571:           mirrorBottom.dy,
572:         );
573:     }
574:     canvas.drawPath(
575:       mirrorPath,
576:       Paint()
577:         ..color = const Color(0xFF2563EB)
578:         ..strokeWidth = 4
579:         ..style = PaintingStyle.stroke,
580:     );
581: 
582:     final objectX = (mirrorX - result.objectDistanceCm * 6)
583:         .clamp(30.0, mirrorX - 20)
584:         .toDouble();
585:     final objectBase = Offset(objectX, cy);
586:     final objectTip = Offset(objectX, cy - 80);
587:     _arrow(canvas, objectBase, objectTip, const Color(0xFF059669));
588: 
589:     if (result.imageDistanceCm != null && result.magnification != null) {
590:       final imageX = (mirrorX + result.imageDistanceCm! * 6)
591:           .clamp(20.0, size.width - 20)
592:           .toDouble();
593:       final imageHeight = -80 * result.magnification!;
594:       final imageBase = Offset(imageX, cy);
595:       final imageTip = Offset(imageX, cy - imageHeight);
596:       _arrow(canvas, imageBase, imageTip, const Color(0xFFDC2626));
597:     }
598:   }
599: 
600:   void _arrow(Canvas canvas, Offset base, Offset tip, Color color) {
601:     final paint = Paint()
602:       ..color = color
603:       ..strokeWidth = 3;
604:     canvas.drawLine(base, tip, paint);
605:     final head = tip.dy > base.dy ? -10 : 10;
606:     canvas.drawLine(Offset(tip.dx - 7, tip.dy + head), tip, paint);
607:     canvas.drawLine(Offset(tip.dx + 7, tip.dy + head), tip, paint);
608:   }
609: 
610:   @override
611:   bool shouldRepaint(covariant _MirrorPainter oldDelegate) =>
612:       oldDelegate.result != result;
613: }

726:     final center = Offset(size.width / 2, size.height / 2);
727:     final radius = size.width / 2;

Now I have a complete understanding of the project structure. Let me create the Class 9 Kaleidoscope feature following the same patterns:

1. Domain: `class9_kaleidoscope_calculator.dart` - Physics calculations for mirror reflections
2. Presentation: `class9_lab_screen.dart` - The UI with kaleidoscope visualization
3. Update `main_screen.dart` - Add navigation button

For the Kaleidoscope with Laws of Reflection concept:
- Multiple mirrors at varying angles
- Show how images are formed by multiple reflections
- Interactive angle control
- Real-time visualization of the kaleidoscope effect

The physics of a kaleidoscope:
- Two mirrors at angle θ create 360/θ images


- With 3+ mirrors, images form in each sector
- The formula for number of images with 2 mirrors is n = (360/θ) - 1 when 360/θ is an integer

I'll build a complete kaleidoscope simulation that lets users adjust mirror angles and count the reflected images in real-time.
Now I'll create the Class 9 Kaleidoscope feature following the established patterns.
